epigraph.text: Democracy is the theory that the common people know what they want, and deserve to get it good and hard.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Florida Man Pays Traffic Ticket with Monopoly Money—Backward Tuxedo Noted
follyOfTheDay.summary:
A Florida gentleman, distinguished by his sartorial commitment to inverted formalwear, appeared at traffic court with a solution born of profound legal innovation: five hundred dollars in colored paper rectangles bearing the visage of Mr. Monopoly himself. When informed that the currency was merely decorative, he protested vigorously that the rules of his preferred board game explicitly stated such tender was 'legal' within that particular jurisdiction. The court, operating under the archaic assumption that legal tender referred to actual legal tender, disagreed. Deputies observed that his jacket was worn not only backward but with conviction—a commitment to the absurd rarely seen in courthouse hallways.

The broader existential question looms: In a civilization where one might mortgage imaginary properties for imaginary currency, is the distinction between Monopoly money and fiat currency truly a matter of degree rather than kind? Our Floridian friend has simply accelerated the inevitable logical endpoint of our monetary system.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: When legal systems meet the operational logic of board games, everyone loses. Except the house. The house always wins.
